This template is e-commerce B2C by default — you must transform it. ### Transformation Checklist (Remove B2C, Add B2B) **REMOVE these from the cloned template:** - `app/tienda/` — product store with cart - `app/envio/` — shipping info - `app/promociones/` — promotions - `app/producto/[slug]/` — individual product pages (replace with /productos/ catalog) - `components/search-autocomplete.tsx` — no product search needed - `components/dark-mode-toggle.tsx` — B2B stays light theme - `components/payment-methods.tsx` — no payments - `components/quick-order.tsx` — no quick ordering - `components/recently-viewed.tsx` — no recently viewed - `components/product-card.tsx` — replace with B2B product card (no price) - `components/hero-carousel.tsx` — single hero image for B2B - `components/promo-carousel.tsx` — no promotions - `components/bottom-nav.tsx` — not needed - `lib/wishlist.ts` and `lib/recently-viewed.ts` — not needed - `lib/hooks/use-wishlist.ts`, `lib/hooks/use-recently-viewed.ts` — remove, update `lib/hooks/index.ts` - `public/images/products/` and `public/images/categories/` — template placeholder images - `public/manifest.json`, `public/sw.js` — PWA files from template **CREATE these new pages:** - `app/productos/page.tsx` — Product catalog with specs, no prices, "Request quote" per item - `app/proyectos/page.tsx` — Portfolio gallery - `app/servicios/page.tsx` — Service listing with process steps - `app/cotizar/page.tsx` — Quote request form → WhatsApp ### @ai-whisperers Package Version Mapping The template pins `^0.1.0` but the GitHub Packages registry has newer versions. When `npm install` fails with `ETARGET` or `401`, check and set exact versions: ```bash # Check available versions (requires GITHUB_TOKEN in env) GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token) npm view @ai-whisperers/admin version ``` | Package | Pinned | Actual latest (as of May 2026) | |---------|--------|-------------------------------| | `@ai-whisperers/ui` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.4.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/theme` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/auth` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/commerce` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/admin` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.1` | | `@ai-whisperers/whatsapp` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/seo` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | | `@ai-whisperers/i18n` | `^0.1.0` | `^0.2.0` | Remove `@ai-whisperers/test-vitest` from devDependencies — it doesn't exist in the registry. ### Content Structure for Blog The template's blog components expect `blog.posts[]` (array under a posts key). If your content JSON has `blog` as a flat array, you must adapt the components: ```tsx // In app/blog/page.tsx — adapt data structure const b = { posts: (c.blog || []), categories: [...new Set((c.blog || []).map((p: any) => p.category).filter(Boolean))], title: "Blog", subtitle: "" } // In app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx const post = ((c.blog || []) as any[]).find((p: any) => p.slug === slug) ``` ### B2B Theme Config Use a light theme with blue/cyan palette for construction/industrial B2B: ```json { "palettes": { "default": { "primary": "#0F62FE", "primaryForeground": "#FFFFFF", "secondary": "#1A1A2E", "secondaryForeground": "#E2E8F0", "accent": "#00A3E0", "background": "#FFFFFF", "surface": "#F8F9FA", "surfaceLight": "#E8ECF0", "text": "#1A1A2E", "textLight": "#4A5568", "textMuted": "#6B7280" } }, "defaultPalette": "default", "theme": "light" } ``` ### SEO Strategy for Zero-Competition Keywords For B2B industrial/construction sites in Paraguay, search for keywords with ZERO existing competition. The market analysis repo has these patterns: ```bash # Find keywords with no competing pages "muro cortina Paraguay" → no rankable results "vidrio fachada Asunción" → no rankable results "vidrio templado Paraguay" → low competition ``` Write 3 blog posts targeting these before launch. Post schedule: one per day for the first 3 days. ### Standard B2B Pages | Page | Purpose | Content | |------|---------|---------| | `/` | Hero + features + product overview + CTA | B2B value props, trust signals | | `/productos` | Technical product catalog | Specs, applications, no prices, "Request quote" CTA per item | | `/proyectos` | Portfolio gallery | Project photos, scope, client names | | `/servicios` | Service listing | Process steps, what each service includes | | `/cotizar` | Quote request form | Name, company, email, phone, project type, product selection, m2, timeline → WhatsApp | | `/nosotros` | Company story | Stats, team, certifications, values | | `/blog` | SEO content | Technical articles, buyer education | | `/contacto` | Contact | Form + phone + WhatsApp + map | | `/faq` | FAQ | Common customer questions | --- ## Sub-type C: Pages Router Standalone (Nexa Paraguay Pattern) A Pages Router Next.js site deployed as a standalone Docker image, with a single locale (ES) and content-driven sections. Images are referenced in `images.json` and resolved at runtime. Used when the client needs a lighter-weight site than the full App Router architecture. ### Architecture ``` repo-root/ ├── src/pages/ │ ├── index.tsx # homepage — getServerSideProps loads content + images │ ├── [slug].tsx # all other pages — SLUG_MAP routes slugs to page configs │ └── admin/content.tsx # optional CMS page ├── src/components/ │ ├── sections.tsx # SECTION_MAP with HeroSection, ProgramsSection, etc. │ ├── content.ts # resolveContent(), resolveImage() helpers │ ├── Header.tsx # renders logo + nav │ └── Footer.tsx ├── nexa-pages/ # page config JSON files (home.json, servicios.json, etc.) ├── content/ │ └── es.json # all site content (single locale) ├── images.json # image manifest — all image paths resolved through this ├── public/images/ # actual image files (brand/, hero/, programs/, etc.) ├── Dockerfile # must include images.json copying at runtime stage ├── docker-compose.yml # Traefik labels at service-level, NOT deploy: labels └── package.json # may use file:/root/ai-whisperers-base/... local deps ``` ### Image Resolution Pattern Content uses `"@src:trust.certificate"` or `"$img": "programs.tierBase"` syntax referencing keys in `images.json`. A helper resolves these at runtime: ```typescript // src/components/content.ts export function resolveImage(images: any, ref: string): string { if (!ref || !images) return '' if (ref.startsWith('@img:') || ref.startsWith('@src:')) { const key = ref.startsWith('@img:') ? ref.replace('@img:', '') : ref.replace('@src:', '') const parts = key.split('.') let obj = images for (const p of parts) { if (obj?.[p]) obj = obj[p] else return '' } return obj?.src || obj?.fallbackSrc || '' } return ref } ``` ### Critical: images.json Must Be in Docker Image The Dockerfile does NOT copy the entire repo — only specific dirs. You MUST add `images.json` explicitly: ```dockerfile COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/images.json ./images.json # ← REQUIRED COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/nexa-pages ./nexa-pages ``` Without this line, `loadJSON(process.cwd(), 'images.json')` returns null at runtime and every image reference resolves to empty string — no images render, no error message. ### Passing Images to Section Components Both `index.tsx` and `[slug].tsx` must: 1. Load `images.json` in `getServerSideProps`: ```typescript const images = loadJSON(process.cwd(), 'images.json')?.images || {} ``` 2. Pass it to the return object: ```typescript return { props: { content, pageConfig, images, pageId: slug, timestamp: Date.now() } } ``` 3. Pass it to section components AND reconstruct pageContent properly: ```tsx if (Component) { const sectionData = resolveContent(content, section.content || section.id) const pc: any = {} const keyName = section.content?.split('.').pop() || section.id if (section.content || section.id) pc[keyName] = sectionData || content return } ``` **Why this matters**: Old `sections.tsx` components access data via `pageContent.hero`, `pageContent.stats`, `pageContent.trust` — they expect the key name to be the LAST segment of the content key (`home.hero` → `hero`). Without this reconstruction using `.split('.').pop()`, the pageContent would use `section.id` (e.g. `"stats-counter"`) which doesn't match what the component looks for (`"stats"`), and ALL section data would be invisible despite the component rendering. **This must be applied identically in both `index.tsx` and `[slug].tsx` (they are near-duplicates).** If `package.json` has `"@ai-whisperers/admin": "file:/root/ai-whisperers-base/..."`, the Docker build fails because the `file:` path doesn't exist inside the container. **Fix:** Build on the VPS host first (`npm install && npm run build`), then build Docker from the already-ready `node_modules`. The `COPY . .` in the Dockerfile picks up the existing `node_modules` directory, and `npm install --legacy-peer-deps` runs against the already-installed deps. This works because npm skips re-installation for packages it can't resolve via `file:` when they're already in `node_modules`. ### SVG Logo Fallback When the client has no designer-provided SVG logo, create one inline: ```svg ``` Create three variants: - `logo.svg` — standard (navy on white background) - `logo-dark.svg` — white text for dark backgrounds (footer) - `logo-icon.svg` — icon-only mark Reference in `images.json`: ```json "logo": { "src": "/images/brand/logo.webp", "srcSvg": "/images/brand/logo.svg", ... } ``` Update `Header.tsx` to render `` instead of plain text `{businessName}`. ### Pitfalls Specific to Pages Router Standalone For a complete deploy checklist covering ALL items in this section plus verification steps, see `references/client-site-launch-checklist.md`. For the section component building pattern (FAQ, Blog, Team, Privacy, Glossary, etc.), see `references/section-components-pattern.md`. For theme standardisation and design-token extraction, see the `src/theme.ts` pattern described below. For a comprehensive code-quality audit checklist covering the 40+ issues found in a production Pages Router site, see `references/pages-router-audit-checklist.md`. - **Theme standardisation**: After building section components, extract all hardcoded hex colors, border-radius values, and spacing into a single `src/theme.ts` file: ```typescript export const theme = { colors: { primary: '#1B2A4A', accent: '#C9A96E', bg: '#F5F5F0', text: '#444', muted: '#666', border: '#e0e0e0' }, radii: { sm: '8px', md: '12px', lg: '16px', full: '50px' }, shadows: { card: '0 4px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)' }, fonts: { body: "'Inter', sans-serif", heading: "'Playfair Display', serif" }, spacing: { section: '4rem 1rem', card: '1.5rem', btn: '0.85rem 2.5rem' }, sizes: { maxWidth: '1200px', contentWidth: '800px' }, } ``` Import in components: `import { theme } from '../theme'` then `style={{ background: theme.colors.primary }}`. Destructure frequently-used keys at top of file: `const c = theme.colors, r = theme.radii, s = theme.spacing`. - **Mobile navigation**: The hamburger button in Header.tsx is often hidden (`display: none`) in the initial scaffold. Enable it with a `@media (max-width: 768px)` CSS-in-JS block that toggles nav visibility via React state: ```tsx ``` - **Footer social links**: site.json typically has `social: { linkedin, instagram }` but the Footer component doesn't render them. Add `socialLinks` to the footer content object and render as `` tags with the site.json social URLs. - **Cache headers for SSR pages**: `getServerSideProps` reads JSON from disk on every request. Add caching via `res.setHeader('Cache-Control', 'public, s-maxage=60, stale-while-revalidate=300')` or return a `headers` key from `getServerSideProps` (Next.js 13+ supports returning `headers` from `getServerSideProps`). - **Navigation hrefs**: The content may use multi-tenant paths like `/s/es/nexa-paraguay/programas`. For standalone deployment, replace with plain paths: `/programas`. Check ALL occurrences in ALL content JSON files (es, en, nl, de) — nav item hrefs, hero CTA links, hero CTA secondary links, blog-related URLs, and ANY href field. A missed multiclient path in content/en.json nav hrefs causes EVERY nav link in the EN locale to 404. The Header component must prepend the locale prefix to nav hrefs (e.g., `/servicios` → `/en/servicios`) after stripping any remaining multiclient prefix. Use a grep across all locale files: `grep -rn '/s/' content/*.json`. - **Placeholder template variables**: Content may have `{{taxRate}}` placeholders. If the component doesn't replace them, pre-replace in the content JSON with the actual value. Search across ALL pages content, not just home. - **Blog post links must include locale prefix**: Blog "Leer más" / "Read more" links in the BlogSection component must prepend the current locale. Without this, blog links on EN/NL/DE pages navigate to `/blog/post-slug` (no locale) which hits the catch-all route without the locale prefix, causing a 404 or wrong-content render. Fix: use `useRouter` from `next/router` and prepend: `href={\`/${locale}/blog/${post.slug}\`}`. The locale variable comes from the page component's `locale` prop, NOT from `router.locale` (which may be undefined in Pages Router). - **Content JSON corruption from patch tool**: The `patch` tool can corrupt large JSON files (208KB, 900+ lines) by misplacing commas. A missing comma between `}` and `\"seo\"` breaks the entire site — every page SSR returns `content: {}` with zero Docker errors. **Always validate after every JSON edit**: `python3 -c \"import json; json.load(open('content/es.json')); print('VALID')\"`. For safety, prefer Python manipulation: `json.load()` + `json.dump()` instead of `patch` or `sed` on large JSON files. Add pre-deploy validation: `for f in content/*.json; do python3 -c \"import json; json.load(open('$f'))\" && echo \"$f: VALID\" || exit 1; done`. - **Docker service labels at service-level**: In `docker-compose.yml`, Traefik labels must be at `services.web.deploy.labels` level, NOT at `services.web.labels`. Docker Compose standalone ignores `deploy:` labels. - **Content items use q/a keys**: FAQ, privacy, and glossary items in content JSON may use `q`/`a` keys instead of `pregunta`/`respuesta` or `question`/`answer` or `term`/`definition`. Support ALL variants in component logic: `item.q || item.pregunta || item.question || item.title` and `item.a || item.respuesta || item.answer || item.description || item.body`. Prefer `q`/`a` first since that's the most common format in the content source. - **Hero CTA hrefs share the same problem**: Nav hrefs aren't the only place multiclient paths hide. Hero CTAs (`home.hero.ctaPrimaryHref`, `home.hero.ctaSecondaryHref`) in EN/NL/DE locale files also use `/s/en/nexa-paraguay/` paths. These cause the hero buttons on non-ES locales to 404. Run a systematic grep: `grep -rn 'cta.*Href' content/*.json | grep '/s/'`. - **Section components receive resolved data directly**: When dispatching to SECTION_MAP, pass `resolveContent(content, section.content)` as `pageContent`, NOT the full page build result. Otherwise the component needs to dig through intermediary keys. Accept both `pageContent` (direct data) and `data` (backward-compat) in component props. - **SECTION_MAP must be in both index.tsx and [slug].tsx**: Both files dispatch section components. Always add new entries to both. - **Rich fallback for [slug].tsx**: The generic section renderer in `[slug].tsx` must handle more than just `headline`/`subheadline`. Add eyebrow text, body content paragraphs, and item grids (for FAQ, glossary, services, blog posts, team members). The renderer should detect `data.items`, `data.groups`, `data.pillars` arrays and render them as card grids. Each item card shows `title`/`pregunta`/`question`/`term` + `description`/`respuesta`/`answer`/`definition`/`body`. - **buildPageContent removal breaks section rendering**: Removing `buildPageContent` is recommended (it's redundant once the dispatch passes sectionData directly), BUT you must reconstruct `pageContent` with the correct key name. Section components in `sections.tsx` access data via `pageContent.hero` — they expect the content key's LAST segment (`home.hero` → `hero`), NOT the section id (`hero` happens to match but `stats-counter` doesn't match `stats`). The dispatch must use `section.content?.split('.').pop()` as the pageContent key. Without this, ALL section data appears invisible despite the component rendering — the hero background image won't load, trust/programs/process images won't appear, and stats won't show values. See the "Passing Images to Section Components" section above for the correct dispatch code. - **Every page config needs SLUG_MAP entry**: If a new page config JSON is added to `nexa-pages/`, it MUST also be added to `SLUG_MAP` in `[slug].tsx` or it will 404. The `SLUG_MAP` is the routing layer between URL slugs and page config filenames. - **image paths in images.json**: After copying `images.json` from the paragu-ai-builder source, ALL paths use `/sites/nexa-paraguay/images/...` prefix which only works in the multi-tenant context. For standalone deployment, batch-replace to `/images/...`. Use a Python script to walk the JSON and fix all `src`, `fallbackSrc`, `fallback`, and `basePath` fields. - **Image path verification**: After fixing paths, verify every referenced image exists on disk with a script. The `images.json` may reference images that don't exist yet. Run `python3 -c "..."` to compare referenced src paths against actual files in `public/images/`. ## Deployment (Both Sub-types) ```yaml # docker-compose.yml services: web: build: . image: :prod ports: ["3000"] networks: [agent-net] deploy: mode: replicated replicas: 2 labels: - "traefik.enable=true" - "traefik.http.routers..entrypoints=websecure" - "traefik.http.routers..rule=Host(`.paragu-ai.com`)" - "traefik.http.routers..tls=true" - "traefik.http.routers..tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver" - "traefik.http.services..loadbalancer.server.port=3000" - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net" healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://127.0.0.1:3000/"] interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 3 start_period: 15s networks: agent-net: external: true ``` **Important:** The template's docker-compose.yml will have hardcoded old values (site name, image name, Traefik rules). Update ALL of them for the new site. ### CI/CD Registration After pushing the repo, add it to ci-cd's `clients.json`: ```json "": { "site": "", "repo": "Ai-Whisperers/", "url": "https://.paragu-ai.com", "type": "nextjs", "pm": "npm", "node": "20", "docker_image": ":prod", "docker_service": "_web", "deploy_strategy": "build-and-scp" } ``` ### DNS (Cloudflare) Add A record in Cloudflare dashboard for paragu-ai.com: - Type: A - Name: `` - Content: `72.61.44.159` - Proxy: Proxied No Cloudflare API key is available in this environment — DNS changes must be done through the Cloudflare dashboard. ## Project Structure (Common) ``` client-site/ ├── app/ │ ├── layout.tsx # providers + metadata + fonts │ ├── page.tsx # home page │ ├── productos/ # (B2B) product catalog │ ├── proyectos/ # (B2B) portfolio │ ├── servicios/ # (B2B) services │ ├── cotizar/ # (B2B) quote form │ ├── blog/ # blog index + [slug] │ ├── nosotros/ # about page │ ├── contacto/ # contact form │ └── faq/ # FAQ accordion ├── components/ # reusable UI components ├── content/ │ ├── es.json # all site content │ └── tokens.json # design tokens ├── Dockerfile ├── docker-compose.yml └── package.json ``` ## Content Management All site copy lives in `content/es.json` (single file). Structure per site type: ### B2B content sections ```json { "home": { "hero": {}, "features": {}, "finalCta": {} }, "products": [{ id, name, description, features[], applications[], cta }], "proyectos": [{ name, location, scope, image }], "servicios": [{ id, title, description, icon }], "nosotros": { story, stats[], values[] }, "faq": [{ question, answer }], "blog": [{ slug, title, excerpt, content }] } ``` ## Multi-Locale Routing (Activation Pattern) All four locales (`es`, `en`, `nl`, `de`) should be activated as infrastructure, not just the default. The pattern: ### Middleware Locale Detection **CRITICAL: Cookie name must match getServerSideProps.** The middleware reads `NEXT_LOCALE` cookie, and getServerSideProps sets it as `NEXT_LOCALE`. If they disagree, Accept-Language header overrides on every visit. Also check `request.cookies.get('locale')` for backward compatibility with any previous middleware version. - **Factory-demo content ships by default**: Fabricated stats (+500 families, 98% success), AI headshots, fake testimonials — these are NOT placeholders the client filled in, they're the scaffold. Replace them before anyone sees the site. Check `is_demo` flag in site.json. // src/middleware.ts const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] const DEFAULT_LOCALE = 'es' export function middleware(request: NextRequest) { const { pathname } = request.nextUrl const firstSegment = pathname.split('/')[1] const hasLocale = LOCALES.includes(firstSegment) if (!hasLocale && !pathname.startsWith('/_next') && !pathname.startsWith('/images') && pathname !== '/favicon.svg') { // Read NEXT_LOCALE first (set by getServerSideProps), fall back to 'locale' (legacy) let preferred = request.cookies.get('NEXT_LOCALE')?.value || request.cookies.get('locale')?.value || DEFAULT_LOCALE if (!LOCALES.includes(preferred)) { const acceptLang = request.headers.get('accept-language') || '' for (const loc of LOCALES) { if (acceptLang.startsWith(loc) || acceptLang.includes(` ${loc}`) || acceptLang.includes(`;${loc}`)) { preferred = loc; break } } } const url = new URL(`/${preferred}${pathname}`, request.url) if (request.nextUrl.search) url.search = request.nextUrl.search return NextResponse.redirect(url, 302) } const result = NextResponse.next() return result } export const config = { matcher: ['/((?!_next|images|favicon\\.svg).*)'], } ``` ### Locale Detection in getServerSideProps — URL Prefix MUST Win **This is the most common multi-locale bug.** The URL prefix (`/es/servicios`) must ALWAYS override cookie and Accept-Language. Without tracking whether the URL explicitly specified a locale, a stale `NEXT_LOCALE=en` cookie will override `/es/`: ```typescript export function getServerSideProps({ params, req, res }: any) { let slug = Array.isArray(params?.slug) ? params.slug.join('/') : (params?.slug || 'home') let locale = 'es' let hasUrlLocale = false const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] // 1) URL prefix locale takes highest priority if (slug.includes('/')) { const parts = slug.split('/') if (LOCALES.includes(parts[0])) { locale = parts[0]; hasUrlLocale = true slug = parts.slice(1).join('/') || 'home' } } else if (LOCALES.includes(slug)) { locale = slug; hasUrlLocale = true; slug = 'home' } // 2) If no URL locale, check cookie (only if URL didn't specify) if (!hasUrlLocale) { const cookieLocale = req?.cookies?.NEXT_LOCALE if (cookieLocale && LOCALES.includes(cookieLocale)) { locale = cookieLocale } } // 3) If still default and no cookie, detect from Accept-Language (only if URL didn't specify) const noCookie = !req?.cookies?.NEXT_LOCALE if (!hasUrlLocale && locale === 'es' && noCookie) { const detected = detectLocaleFromHeader(req?.headers?.['accept-language']) if (detected) locale = detected } // Set locale cookie for persistence const cookieMaxAge = 60 * 60 * 24 * 365 res.setHeader('Set-Cookie', `NEXT_LOCALE=${locale}; Path=/; Max-Age=${cookieMaxAge}; SameSite=Lax`) // ... } ``` ### Header: Nav Hrefs Must Prepend Locale Prefix After stripping any multiclient prefix (`/s/en/nexa-paraguay/`), prepend the current locale to every nav href. The language switcher must also be in the header — implement a dropdown with flag emoji + language code: ```tsx // In Header.tsx const LOCALE_FLAGS = { es: '🇪🇸 ES', en: '🇬🇧 EN', nl: '🇳🇱 NL', de: '🇩🇪 DE' } const currentLocale = locale || 'es' function switchLocale(newLocale: string) { const path = router.asPath const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] let cleanPath = path const parts = path.split('/').filter(Boolean) if (parts.length > 0 && LOCALES.includes(parts[0])) { cleanPath = '/' + parts.slice(1).join('/') || '/' } window.location.href = '/' + newLocale + cleanPath } // Nav item href rendering let href = item.href || '#' // Strip existing multiclient prefix href = href.replace(/^\/s\/[^/]+\/[^/]+/, '') // Prepend locale prefix for internal links if (!href.startsWith('http') && !href.startsWith('/' + currentLocale)) { href = '/' + currentLocale + href } ``` ### Locale JSON Key Parity Verification Write a comparison script (`scripts/compare-locale-keys.py`) that walks key paths across all locale JSONs using dot-separated paths and finds truly missing keys (not structural differences). Add empty strings for missing keys: ```python import json def walk(obj, path=''): results = set() if isinstance(obj, dict): for k, v in obj.items(): p = f'{path}.{k}' if path else k results.add(p) results |= walk(v, p) elif isinstance(obj, list) and obj and isinstance(obj[0], dict): for i, item in enumerate(obj): results |= walk(item, path) else: results.add(path) return results ``` ### Catch-All Route [[...slug]] Instead of [slug] To support `/en/faq` style multi-segment URLs, use `[[...slug]]` NOT `[slug]`: ```bash mv src/pages/"[slug].tsx" src/pages/"[[...slug]].tsx" ``` This captures `/` → empty array, `/faq` → `['faq']`, `/en/faq` → `['en', 'faq']`. `index.tsx` CANNOT coexist — delete it and handle the home page case within `[[...slug]]`. ### Locale Extraction ```typescript export function getServerSideProps({ params }: any) { let slug = Array.isArray(params?.slug) ? params.slug.join('/') : (params?.slug || 'home') let locale = 'es' const LOCALES = ['es', 'en', 'nl', 'de'] if (slug.includes('/')) { const parts = slug.split('/') if (LOCALES.includes(parts[0])) { locale = parts[0]; slug = parts.slice(1).join('/') || 'home' } } const fullContent = loadJSON(process.cwd() + '/content', `${locale}.json`) || ... } ``` ### Locale JSON Key Parity Before activating, audit all locale JSON files against the reference (`es.json`). Walk every key and check it exists in each locale. Add missing keys with empty strings (placeholders until translated). The gaps are typically 10-15 keys per locale: new pages not propagated, recently-added content keys, object sub-keys. ### Verification ```bash for loc in es en nl de; do for page in "" faq sobre servicios programas contacto blog privacidad glosario; do curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://site.com/${loc}/${page}" done done ``` ## Asset Activation Policy Nothing gets deleted without client confirmation. Every dormant asset is an unactivated feature. Keep all `@ai-whisperers/*` packages installed (tree-shaken at build time, zero runtime cost). Keep unreferenced images on disk and in the manifest (pre-generated for future campaigns, <1MB total). #### Content JSON Integrity Check (Pre-Deploy) Before building the Docker image, validate ALL content JSON files. A corrupt JSON makes SSR return `content: {}` (empty pages) with zero errors in Docker logs: ```bash python3 -c " import json, os for f in os.listdir('content'): if f.endswith('.json'): try: json.load(open(f'content/{f}')) except json.JSONDecodeError as e: print(f'CORRUPT: {f} — {e}') exit(1) print('All content JSON valid') " ``` ### Stale .next/ directory pitfall After `git pull origin main` that changes content JSON files or source code, the existing `.next/` directory may be stale. The Docker `COPY . .` includes `.next/`, so stale build output + new content = empty SSR pages. Fix: ```bash rm -rf .next/ # Only when source/content files changed npm run build # Rebuild from scratch ``` After any content edit or page config change, validate all section content keys resolve: ```python import json, os with open('content/es.json') as f: content = json.load(f) for fname in os.listdir('nexa-pages'): cfg = json.load(open(f'nexa-pages/{fname}')) for sec in cfg.get('sections', []): ck = sec.get('content', sec['id']) resolved = content for part in ck.split('.'): if isinstance(resolved, dict) and part in resolved: resolved = resolved[part] else: resolved = None; break if resolved is None: print(f"MISSING: {fname} → {sec['id']} → {ck}") ``` ## Client Site Deep-Dive — Full Business Assessment When a client site already exists with docs (like Nexa Paraguay), don't just look at code — produce a structured assessment that covers ALL areas the business owner needs to see. ### Assessment Structure 1. **Code & Content Audit** — browse the live site + examine repo structure + read all docs 2. **Competitive Position** — price positioning, AI readiness, niche focus (use existing docs if they exist) 3. **Financial Model Review** — unit economics, funnel estimates, cost structure (which docs exist) 4. **Gap Analysis** — what's broken now (P0/P1) vs what needs client input 5. **Phased Roadmap** — organize into 6 phases by impact: (1) Fix broken pages, (2) Conversion path, (3) Trust & credibility, (4) SEO & analytics, (5) Multi-language, (6) AI differentiation ### Quick-Win Batch Sizing Always include a "quick-wins I can execute right now" section with concrete, batch-fixable items: - Fix page config `id` fields across all page JSONs - Add sitemap.xml + robots.txt - Replace fabricated stats with honest text - Fix blog image key mismatches - Remove `is_demo` flag - Add unique meta titles per page - Enable additional locale routing ### Business Model Impact Table Show the before/after for the client in financial terms they understand: | Metric | Current | With improvements | |---|---|---| | Clients/month | X | Y | | Avg ticket | $Z | $W | | Monthly revenue | $A | $B | ### Key Questions to Answer - What's the AI differentiation opportunity? (Check competitor landscape — most have zero AI) - What justifies the premium pricing? (AI layer, multilingual, faster process) - What's the head start window? (Usually 12-18 months before competitors catch up) - What are the 3-5 blog posts that would dominate zero-competition keywords? ## Pitfalls See `references/post-deployment-audit-pattern.md` for the systematic audit process to run after every deployment. - **Factory-demo content ships by default**: Fabricated stats (+500 families, 98% success), AI headshots, fake testimonials — these are NOT placeholders the client filled in, they're the scaffold. Replace them before anyone sees the site. Check `is_demo` flag in site.json.**: The `template-nextjs-client` has 52 files that must be removed for B2B. Missing any will cause build failures. Run the transformation checklist above. - **@ai-whisperers package versions**: Always resolve version mismatches before building. Use `GITHUB_TOKEN=$(gh auth token)` for npm install. - **Network name**: Always use `agent-net` for Swarm stacks. Verify with `docker network ls | grep overlay`. - **Build output**: The Next.js `output: 'standalone'` config is required for Docker builds. Set in `next.config.ts`. - **Blog data structure**: Template expects `blog.posts[]`. If your content has flat `blog[]`, adapt both `app/blog/page.tsx` and `app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx`. - **docker-compose.yml stale values**: The template's compose file has `superspuma` hardcoded in image name and Traefik labels. Replace ALL references. - **Content JSON has Fun4Me remnants**: The template pre-populates `content/es.json` with erotic/Fun4Me products, images, blog posts, brand references, Instagram handles (@fun4me_py), and a `brand` section. Overwrite completely with client content. Also check these files for hardcoded Fun4Me values: - `components/share-whatsapp.tsx` — hardcoded "Mirá esto de Fun4Me:" text - `components/article-json-ld.tsx` — hardcoded `fun4me.paragu-ai.com` URL - `components/breadcrumb-jsonld.tsx` — hardcoded `fun4me.paragu-ai.com` URL - `components/faq-json-ld.tsx` — reads `item.q`/`item.a` but content has `question`/`answer` - `app/rss.xml/route.ts` — "Fun4Me Store" title and "bienestar íntimo" description - `app/contacto/page.tsx` — Quick links for order status, returns, shipping; Instagram @fun4me_py; hardcoded Fun4Me phone - `app/nosotros/page.tsx` — "¿Por qué Fun4Me?" section with sex shop story; reads `c.brand` instead of `c.nosotros` - **Dead index files**: After removing components, check `lib/hooks/index.ts` and similar index files for dangling re-exports. - **AnalyticsProvider API**: `@ai-whisperers/seo` v0.2.0 has a different API than the template expects. The `AnalyticsProvider` component from the package may be empty or mismatched. If build fails, remove the import and use raw GA4 script tag instead. - **DNS can't be configured from this environment**: Cloudflare API token not available. Must add A records through the Cloudflare web dashboard. - **No prices on B2B sites**: B2B products are custom-quoted. Never show prices. Use "Request quote" CTAs instead of "Buy now". - **WhatsApp as primary CTA**: For B2B in Paraguay, WhatsApp is the dominant business communication tool. Every page should have a WhatsApp float + at least one WhatsApp CTA section. - **Mobile sticky WhatsApp bar**: Add `components/mobile-cta.tsx` and include in layout. Add `pb-20 md:pb-0` to body className for spacing. - **Docker build with GITHUB_TOKEN**: Using `--build-arg GITHUB_TOKEN` triggers Docker BuildKit secret warnings and fails. Instead use `Dockerfile.prod` that copies the prebuilt `.next/standalone` directory and runs `node_modules/.bin/next start` — no npm install at Docker build time. Build on the host first, then Docker just packages output. - **CI/CD reusable workflow failures**: The `Ai-Whisperers/ci-cd` reusable workflows may not be accessible from new repos due to org permissions. Use a standalone `deploy.yml` that SSHes to the VPS and runs the deploy commands directly. This avoids the ci-cd access issue entirely. - **FAQ JSON-LD format mismatch**: If FAQ data uses `question`/`answer` keys but the JSON-LD component reads `q`/`a`, it silently fails to render FAQ schema. Fix by updating the component's key access. - **RSS feed inherits template owner**: The template's `app/rss.xml/route.ts` has hardcoded site name, description, and base URL. Always update all three for the new client. - **All JSON-LD components hardcode fun4me URL**: Every `*-json-ld.tsx` component needs its base URL updated. Read from `content.site.url` instead of hardcoding. {"success": true, "name": "professional-services-website", "file": "references/[REDACTED_TOKEN].md", "content": "# Nexa Paraguay — Visual & UX Audit, May 7 2026

## Team Portraits (5 + 1 group)
Labelled "staging placeholders" in images.json alt text but displayed as real team on /es/sobre.
Visual inspection determined they are stock photo models, not real team members.
Operations Director: clean-cut man in navy suit, bookshelf background, gray temples — generic "executive" stock
Commercial Director: woman in navy blazer, Amsterdam canal view — generic "European businesswoman"
Legal Lead: woman in glasses with certificate wall — stock photo cliché
Accounting Lead, Client Success: more stock models
Group shot: not displayed on page, labeled "pending real photo"

**Impact:** When a prospect recognizes a stock photo from another site, credibility is destroyed.

## Stock Photo Inventory
- Why Paraguay (6 cards: jacaranda street, cafe with lake view, government building) — actually good, could be Paraguay
- Process icons (hands typing, hands passport, phone, laptop) — recycled stock hands, no narrative
- Programs (passport+flower, wallet, city view, green field) — tierras/field image most authentic
- Trust signals (documents, certificates, ID, building) — acceptable for official documents
- Blog (8 images: cost of living, properties, neighborhoods etc.) — generic but fine for blog
- Hero section — dark navy gradient, no actual photo, underwhelming for a USD 2,900+ service

## Missing Visuals
- Testimonial photos exist in images.json (5 clients + 5 posters) but never render on any page
- No real Paraguay-specific imagery (Asunción skyline, Costanera sunset)
- No real office/team photos

## UX Bugs Found & Fixed
1. EN locale nav ALL 404 — multiclient /s/en/nexa-paraguay/ paths in content/en.json
2. ES "Reubicación" nav → /sobre (About Us) instead of /programas
3. Blog "Leer más" links → 404 (no locale prefix)
4. /es/prensa completely blank — page config referenced wrong content key
5. /es/casos-de-exito empty — no content
6. Locale cookie mismatch: middleware read `locale` cookie, getServerSideProps set `NEXT_LOCALE`
7. URL prefix locale overridden by stale cookie — no `hasUrlLocale` tracking
8. NL/DE `{{taxRate}}` template placeholders never filled

## Visual Fixes Needed (Client Action)
1. Replace team portraits with real photos (phone camera headshots > stock)
2. Add 1-2 real Paraguay photos (skyline, tereré in park)
3. Remove "staging placeholder" alt text or replace the images
4. Render or remove testimonial images (5 unused in images.json)
5. Replace hero gradient with actual photo of Asunción or client meeting
", "file_type": ".md"}

Now I have the full picture. Let me formalize the plan.

---

## Nexa Paraguay — Complete Content & UX Improvement Plan

### Problem summary (what's duplicated or unnecessary)

| # | Issue | Pages affected | Severity |
|---|-------|---------------|----------|
| 1 | **Services grid** — Same 9 services (same names, same groups, same descriptions) on both pages | `/en/` + `/en/servicios` | High |
| 2 | **Process timeline** — Same 5 steps, same descriptions on both pages | `/en/` + `/en/proceso` | High |
| 3 | **Programs/pricing** — Same 4 tiers with same prices on both pages | `/en/` + `/en/programas` | Medium |
| 4 | **About/Founder story** — Same 3-paragraph "Our Story" on both pages | `/en/sobre` + `/en/fundador` | Medium |
| 5 | **Trust signals** — `home.trust` (4 items: program/trip/team/pricing) is redundant when `/en/servicios` and `/en/sobre` have more detailed versions | `/en/` overlaps with `/en/servicios` and `/en/sobre` | Low |

### What I recommend

**For #1 (Services):** Remove the "Our Services" section from the homepage. It has 9 items with short descriptions — the canonical `/servicios` page has the full detail with benefit checklists. The homepage already sells the value proposition through `trust-signals`, `programs-comparison`, and `why-destination`. Removing `services` from `home.json` eliminates the duplication without losing anything a visitor actually needs on the landing page.

**For #2 (Process):** Replace the homepage process timeline with a condensed 3-step summary (not 5 steps) — make it teaser content that points to `/proceso` for the full week-by-week. Specifically: remove `process-timeline` from `home.json`, add a lighter section that says "Learn about our 5-step process →" linking to the dedicated page. The 5-step timeline with full descriptions belongs on the process page only.

**For #3 (Programs):** The homepage programs section is valuable as a "what you can buy" quick overview. Keep it, but add a visual distinction — show only 3 tiers (drop Land Purchase from homepage since it's quote-based and niche) in a horizontal row instead of 4 matching the detail page exactly. The `/programas` page keeps all 4.

**For #4 (About/Founder):** Remove the `story` section from `/fundador`. The founder page should be a personal narrative ("why I started this", founder background) — not a repeat of the company story. Replace with a founder bio/introduction section that's unique.

**For #5 (Trust signals):** Already minimal overlap (different content paths). No action needed.

### Summary

| Change | File(s) to edit | Effort |
|--------|----------------|--------|
| Remove `services` section from `home.json` | `nexa-pages/home.json` | 2 min |
| Condense process on homepage (remove `process-timeline`, add teaser CTA or keep as 3-step) | `nexa-pages/home.json` + optional new section | 5 min |
| Remove Land Purchase tier from homepage programs (keep 3) | `/content/en.json` (and nl/de/es) `home.programs.tiers[]` | 5 min |
| Replace `/fundador` story with unique founder bio | `/content/en.json` `founderPage.story` → rewrite as personal bio | 10 min |
| Add Cache-Control header to SSR | `src/pages/[[...slug]].tsx` | 2 min |

- **faq**, **home**, **por-que-paraguay**, **proceso**, **programas**, **sobre** — all say "Take the first step / Book a free 30-minute consultation / Book free consultation" — **exact same text on 6 pages**

- **por-que-paraguay**, **sobre**, **trust** — all show: "One program", "One trip", "One team", "Transparent pricing" — **identical across 3 pages**

All different content for context. **Not a duplicate.**

### NEWSLETTER (2 pages)
- **blog** and **recursos** — same content path `resourcesPage.newsletter`. **Identical.**
